title: podman build --mount=bind can't access contents of previous stage when stage is cached
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

`podman build` with a `RUN --mount=type=bind` to a previous stage can't access contents of previous stage if stage is cached. Using `buildah` directly works fine (or I would have reported there)

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
Using this `Dockerfile`:
```Dockerfile
FROM quay.io/centos/centos:7 AS common
RUN echo "common" > /common.txt

FROM common AS build
RUN echo "build" > /build.txt

FROM quay.io/centos/centos:7
ARG NONCE
RUN --mount=type=bind,from=build,target=/build \
    set -ex; \
    cat /build/common.txt; \
    cat /build/build.txt; \
    : ;
```

1. `podman build --build-arg NONCE="$(date +%N)" -f - < ./Dockerfile`
2. `podman build --build-arg NONCE="$(date +%N)" -f - < ./Dockerfile`

**Describe the results you received:**

Second build results in:
```text
[3/3] STEP 3/3: RUN --mount=type=bind,from=build,target=/build     set -ex;     cat /build/common.txt;     cat /build/build.txt;     : ;
+ cat /build/common.txt
common
+ cat /build/build.txt
cat: /build/build.txt: No such file or directory
Error: building at STEP "RUN --mount=type=bind,from=build,target=/build set -ex;     cat /build/common.txt;     cat /build/build.txt;     : ;": while running runtime: exit status 1
```



**Describe the results you expected:**

Build should succeed and should show "build" in the output. Using `buildah` directly works:

*First Run:*
```shell
$ buildah version
Version:         1.26.2
Go Version:      go1.17.12
Image Spec:      1.0.2-dev
Runtime Spec:    1.0.2-dev
CNI Spec:        1.0.0
libcni Version:  v1.1.0
image Version:   5.21.1
Git Commit:
Built:           Tue Aug  2 03:54:09 2022
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
BuildPlatform:   linux/amd64

$ buildah build --build-arg NONCE="$(date +%N)" -f - < ./Dockerfile
[1/3] STEP 1/2: FROM quay.io/centos/centos:7 AS common
[1/3] STEP 2/2: RUN echo "common" > /common.txt
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 174f56854903 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 0087af90d74d done
Copying config 8cc3db1af1 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
--> 8cc3db1af16
[2/3] STEP 1/2: FROM 8cc3db1af160ad40e954fdc634b8cccb7cd9930ab19521482cf92bda0b31116b AS build
[2/3] STEP 2/2: RUN echo "build" > /build.txt
[3/3] STEP 1/3: FROM quay.io/centos/centos:7
[3/3] STEP 2/3: ARG NONCE
[3/3] STEP 3/3: RUN --mount=type=bind,from=build,target=/build     set -ex;     cat /build/common.txt;     cat /build/build.txt;     : ;
+ cat /build/common.txt
+ cat /build/build.txt
+ :
common
build
[3/3] COMMIT
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 174f56854903 skipped: already exists
Copying blob eb1c6077e9c3 done
Copying config 64863f605a done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
--> 64863f605a8
64863f605a81fef9fd150ee1fa27f90a07648ddd5290e8b2e37674e6a571aa16
```

*Second Run:*
```shell
$ buildah build --build-arg NONCE="$(date +%N)" -f - < ./Dockerfile
[1/3] STEP 1/2: FROM quay.io/centos/centos:7 AS common
[1/3] STEP 2/2: RUN echo "common" > /common.txt
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 174f56854903 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 6c757404b868 done
Copying config b5135726bf done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
--> b5135726bfb
[3/3] STEP 1/3: FROM quay.io/centos/centos:7
[3/3] STEP 2/3: ARG NONCE
[3/3] STEP 3/3: RUN --mount=type=bind,from=build,target=/build     set -ex;     cat /build/common.txt;     cat /build/build.txt;     : ;
[2/3] STEP 1/2: FROM b5135726bfb564456eec502114d639ee279be72fc710a2a2434cc8ddd47ed4fe AS build
[2/3] STEP 2/2: RUN echo "build" > /build.txt
+ cat /build/common.txt
common
build
+ cat /build/build.txt
+ :
[3/3] COMMIT
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 174f56854903 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 0413ff47e620 done
Copying config dbd6c37c81 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
--> dbd6c37c815
dbd6c37c8158906bc2acc8cca0d278c1ec67fe2d1e3acf6a7e580fb3d389fc2f
```


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Docker also handles it correctly.

**Output of `podman version`:**

Note: We've duplicated this on 4.1.1 and 4.3.0 on Ubuntu 20.04 and Rocky 8
```
$ podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.3.0
API Version:  4.3.0
Go Version:   go1.19
Git Commit:   ad42af94903ce4f3c3cd0693e4e17e4286bf094b-dirty
Built:        Fri Oct 21 18:20:57 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```
$ podman info
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman` or `brew info podman`):**

Note: this doesn't match the build above because we wanted to test with 4.3.0 so we built it by hand
```
$ rpm -q podman
podman-4.1.1-2.module+el8.6.0+997+05c9d812.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
Nevermind, it _is_ a buildah bug, I just didn't realize buildah doesn't cache by default so my repro steps weren't hitting it.

I'll reopen this over there.
